fundamentals.name: ASML Holding N.V. - New York Re

judgment.summary: ASML demonstrates robust growth and profitability with strong margins and free cash flow, though it trades at a premium valuation.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimate by 8.0%; next earnings date is 2026-07-15.
positives: Revenue growth 13.2% YoY and Earnings growth 19.2% YoY indicate strong top and bottom-line expansion.
Gross margin 52.6%, Operating margin 36.0%, and Profit margin 29.7% show excellent operational efficiency.
Return on equity 52.2% and Return on assets 15.7% reflect superior capital utilization.
Free cash flow $8.24B (TTM) is substantial, supporting operations and shareholder returns.
Analyst consensus is 'strong buy' with a mean target price suggesting upside potential.
negatives: Trailing P/E 51.96 and Forward P/E 32.90 indicate a high valuation, pricing in significant future growth.
PEG ratio 2.32 suggests the stock may be overvalued relative to its earnings growth.
Price/Book 1368.60 and Price/Sales 17.87 are exceptionally high, typical for growth stocks but warranting caution.
Debt/Equity 12.99 is high, though offset by strong cash flow and profitability.
Quick ratio 0.69 suggests potential short-term liquidity concerns if inventory cannot be sold quickly.

news.paragraph: ASML Holding NV is highlighted as a top tech stock within billionaire Ken Fisher's portfolio, suggesting positive investor sentiment and confidence in its market position. While no specific catalysts are mentioned, this endorsement from a prominent investor could influence market perception. Traders should consider the implications of such high-profile backing as they evaluate options strategies.

headlines.title: This is Why ASML Holding NV (ASML) is One of the Top Tech Stocks in Billionaire Ken Fisher’s Portfolio
